claim: Galgos from Spanish rescue programs 'almost universally' have experienced maltreatment and neglect — the trauma baseline is not an exceptional case but the statistical norm.
stance_target: galgo trauma baseline should be treated as default prior (P≈1.0), not a case-by-case variable; all rescue galgos in Algarve should be modeled as high-trauma individuals
scope_conditions: galgos rescued from Spain by US/international adoption organizations
quoted_support: these dogs have almost universally seen maltreatment and neglect, these dogs may come with additional veterinary and behavioral costs.

claim: Galgos require minimum 5-foot fence (preferably 6-foot) for containment — organizations enforce this as a hard adoption requirement, not a recommendation.
stance_target: galgo vertical escape capacity is high enough that 5-foot fence is the floor, not the ceiling, for containment; search area modeling must account for this
scope_conditions: galgo adoption requirements; all adoption homes in Minnesota Greyhound Rescue program
quoted_support: We will only consider homes for our Galgos with a MINIMUM 5-foot fence (prefer 6-foot, or leashed walking only).

claim: Up to 60,000 galgos are discarded annually in Spain, representing over 10% of all abandoned animals — confirming the scale of the trauma-baseline population that feeds Algarve rescue networks.
stance_target: galgo population in Iberian rescue system (including Algarve) has a structurally high trauma baseline due to scale of annual abandonment
quoted_support: Some organizations estimate the number may reach 60,000 Galgos discarded annually. Over 10% of all animals abandoned in Spain are Galgos.
numbers: over 10% of all abandoned animals in Spain
